sections.paragraph:
ἀνέρχομαι pres. 2 sg. ἀνέρχεσαι (ApcSed 11:16 p. 134, 35 Ja.); fut. ἀνελεύσεται (Just., A I, 54, 7f); 2 aor. ἀνῆλθον; pf. ἀνελήλυθα (Just., Tat.) (s. ἔρχομαι; Hom.+) to move from a lower to a higher place, go up, come up (X., Hell. 2, 4, 39 εἰς τ. ἀκρόπολιν; 3 Km 13:12; Jos., Ant. 6, 314; Just., A I, 21, 3) εἰς τὸ ὄρος J 6:3 (on the journey to Jerusalem s. ἀναβαίνω 1aα) ἀ. εἰς Ἱεροσόλυμα Gal 1:17f (of journey to the capital Epict. 1, 11, 32 νῦν ἐν Ῥώμῃ ἀνέρχῃ; PTebt 412, 3 ἄνελθε εἰς μητρόπολιν; 411, 5; Jos., Ant. 16, 91 εἰς τ. Ῥώμην). Of coming up out of a river ἀ. ἀπὸ ὕδατος GEb 18, 36.—M-M.

pres. pres. = present

  1. sg. = singular

ApcSed ApcSed = Apocalypse of Sedrach—List 2

fut. fut. = fut.

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pf. = perfect

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

GEb GEb = Gospel of the Ebionites—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 77
